author: @collectorking6204
text: I'm a game dev. I have years of experience working in AAA. Take what I say with a pinch of salt because I am not doxing myself. Corporate is horrible. It's full of people that don't care about what they are working on and they don't care about the people that buy it. These people are talentless hacks that can not create anything. The gaming industry is in the state that it's in because of these morons. It's the same for comics, tv and movies. All you need to do is spend one week to a month in this environment and you will know why. Anyone who cares about doing a good job is pushed out by yes-men, people who want to climb the corporate ladder and clowns that don't have any actual talent but know how to play the corporate game. It's a joke.

replies.author: @Launchpad05
replies.text: We need to make our own IPs, and never sell them to the corporate clowns.

replies.author: @Nunnyahbizhen
replies.text: That's the facts with anything that becomes "corporate". They talk a lot of good speak but the product produced speaks for itself. If you care about anything you have to take a chance on yourself and be wise about the outlook, and if you start making a splash look out because then the corpo sharks are coming for you!

replies.author: @Malgrim89
replies.text: That’s those people’s entire profession. Starting from school. It’s all about the money for them. You can’t be surprised about this.

​@WagsquagThat's how you do it. Look how many games we've already sent down the toilet that they arrogantly paraded in our faces expecting us to eat up the slop. Money talks. Bullshit walks.

author: @a_saint_i_aint
text:
The U.S. comic book industry is dying.

Book publishers have abandoned the mass market paperback format.

There is almost nothing that interests me on TV.

I know nothing lasts forever, but did all of the good stuff have to die while I am alive?

replies.author: @jonarbuckle778
replies.text:
You just have to find new stuff to like. The west is going through a creatively bankrupt stage right now, but other countries are doing pretty damn well. Japan and Korea for example. Then of course you could always look for shows, movies, books made decades ago that you haven't watched or read yet.

Point is, there is still a lot of good entertainment to be found and enjoyed if you look hard enough.

replies.author: @quad7778
replies.text: This is something I learned from the IP Battletech. The IP will live on, it may change hands and company but it'll live on as long as people keep sharing the stuff that's good to keep it alive. Then maybe one day the IP will make a resurgence because enough people are interested in what was good.

author: @smallcd
text: *3 words that spell the end of creativity: Private Equity Group.*

replies.author: @Launchpad05
replies.text: Never let A private equity group take over your business.

replies.author: @Cyborg647
replies.text: Does steam fall under this label ? Or is it the equity part i dont quite get

replies.author: @ForlanceAbice
replies.text: @Cyborg647 Steam is a private company, funded mostly by their revenue gained by Steam purchases, whereas a public company are funded by investors that control the direction of how the company is handled. So no, Steam cannot be affected in such a direct manner. But they can be hit in other ways, such as the lawsuits in the UK and New York to pressure them to change their tune, or via payment cards and processors like Paypal, Visa and MasterCard.

replies.author: @smallcd
replies.text:
@Cyborg647 It's more when the Private Equity Group buys an asset with no prior association to the asset (Valve Corp. was formerly Valve Software so it's always been Gabe Newell).
Think of it more as a company that buys another company and slowly but surely makes changes that may benefit (or hinder) the purchased company.

author: @dbsommers1
text: Funny thing. Nowadays, if you're setting up a business with and hire an advisor, they always ask 'What is your exit strategy?'. Meaning at what point do you sell out to someone bigger. You're not expected to run your successful company for years, you're expected to sell it at a profit.

author: @kurtbrown2706
text: Dark Hose does not own Hellboy. A lot of their stuff is famously creator owned like Sin City, Usagi Yojimbo, The Goon, etc. I think Dark Horse might have Umbrella Academy and The Mask as far as bigger hitters might go. I do agree they're mining IP, but it's not as big of a haul as it might look.

author: @andrewtaylor940
text: Regarding comparisons to the  Skydance WBD buyout. The difference is Embracer went on a buying spree based on the promise of other peoples money. A $2 Billion Suadi investment deal. Then that deal fell through. And thats when they had to start sladhing and burning. Whereas over at Skydance, the Ellison's have the cash to do the deals. You will see a lit of consolidating between Paramount and WB. And they unquestionably will be cutting costs across the boards. But those deals do not involve external financing or investment. And only involve less that 30% the Ellison's net worth. It's a much more stable situation than the Embracer mess.

author: @xenogorwraithblade2538
text: I stopped reading new comics a little over a decade, decade and a half ago. Sometimes I'll go and read a classic arc or two from the good old days, but comics were dying before even tabletop got corrupted. It started by things just getting more bland, sanding off all the edges, softening themes and language to help produce and subsequently protect a softer people. Then The Message™ started popping up more and more often (I'd already checked out by this point). Now the only comics I read came out in years that start with 1 unless it's something really special like The Goon. I oppose the enshittification of everything by vowing to give every bit of new slop nothing but the most vitriolic, foul mouthed ire that my lethargic ass can muster.

author: @jmorton201
text: Dark Horse Comics not just superheroes.

author: @jmorton201
text: Met him years ago the marvel dc folk were too stuck up but there was Mike happy to talk. He took risks. He provided support for new creatives. Broke the Marvel DC hold. Got me onto Chevel noir, next men, Hard boiled, Hellboy. Did a better sequel to Aliens than all of the movie dross. Another parasitic organism drains the creative blood out of another indy entity. Comics have been dead for years and this just proves it. Who else would get a promo for their Dark Horse Presents 5th Anniversary Cover. After all Dark Horse Presents played a significant role in the early development of Army of Darkness comics by publishing the initial three-issue adaptation of the film in 1992

author: @RyanRoemer8624
text: Embracer needs to be cancelled

author: @apexcomix3200
text: Too much of a spending spree is never wise. I predict Embracer could be bought out in five years. Mike Richardson needs to retire anyway and enjoy his time.

author: @Murlock1986
text: Sighs, the only comic book that’s making an impact is absolute comic books. And what disturbs the most is the scalpers that are ruining that collection culture especially when you see the nonsense going on in Pokémon card world. I’m am sick of people destroying what used to be a niche nerd world all for inclusivity and removing gatekeeping so feelings doing get hurt. Creativity used to mean you could mock the peers who didn’t understand it in nerd culture. Everything is dying from the corpo touch and dei people
